testPageVerification.details: Test page contains 6 takt/tabs block instances with 6 takt/tabs-item sets. All 6 spec variations found: Full Content (Yellow), Blue Accent, Minimal Content, Many Tabs (Orange), Long Content (Purple), Green Accent.

build.details: webpack compiled with 3 warnings (CSS parser warnings for custom gap variables, asset size warnings). No TypeScript or compilation errors.

desktop.tablist: role=tablist with aria-orientation=vertical present on all 6 sections
desktop.tabButtons: role=tab with aria-selected, aria-controls, tabindex (0 for active, -1 for inactive) on all tab buttons
desktop.tabPanels: role=tabpanel with aria-labelledby, tabindex=0 on all tab panels
desktop.keyboardNavigation: ArrowUp/ArrowDown for navigation, Home/End for first/last, Enter/Space for activation implemented in Tabs.js

mobile.accordionButtons: aria-expanded attribute present on all accordion buttons
mobile.accordionContent: role=region with aria-labelledby on all accordion content areas

details.semanticHtml: section element as wrapper, button elements for interactive controls, proper heading hierarchy via ThemeHeading

markupDuplication.details: PHP uses separate desktop (hidden md:flex) and mobile (md:hidden) structures. This is intentionally documented and architecturally necessary: desktop uses ARIA tablist pattern while mobile uses accordion/button+region pattern. Mobile accordion content is populated via JS content cloning from desktop panels. TSX uses a single responsive structure since the editor doesn't need the mobile accordion pattern. This structural difference is justified.

details.wrapperElement: Both use <section> with 'tabs py-10 sm:py-16' classes
details.container: Both use 'container grid grid-cols-1 gap-y-10 sm:gap-y-16'
details.themeHeading: Both use ThemeHeading/get_template_part with headingSize=3, columns=2
details.tabButtonClasses: TSX uses responsive classes (rounded-3xl md:rounded-l-3xl md:rounded-r-none md:border-r-0) for single DOM structure; PHP uses split structures with desktop-only (border-l border-t border-b rounded-l-3xl) and mobile-only (rounded-3xl border) classes. Functionally equivalent.
details.contentPanel: Both use bg-accent-lighter rounded-3xl p-6 w-full for the content panel
details.tabsContainer: Both use tabs-container class for inner blocks wrapper
details.childBlock: Both use min-h-10 wrapper with prose wide-prose no-edge-margin inner div
details.note: Structural divergence between TSX (single responsive) and PHP (split desktop/mobile) is architecturally justified for different ARIA patterns

variation1_yellow.heading: Still Looking for Support That Fits Your Student Life?

variation5_longContent.heading: Comprehensive Guide to Student Services at IGNITE

details.tabDescriptions: Present on variations with tabDescription data (variations 1, 2, 5, 6). Descriptions hidden by default, shown on active tab via JS.
details.tabsHeadingSync: Parent tabsHeading arrays match child tabTitle attributes in raw content for all variations.
details.tabsDescriptionSync: Parent tabsDescription arrays match child tabDescription attributes in raw content for all variations.

issues.description: CRLF line endings in Tabs.tsx - should be LF
CRLF line endings in TabsItem.tsx - should be LF

Mobile accordion content in PHP is empty on initial render and populated via JS cloning (Tabs.js clonePanelContentToAccordion). With JavaScript disabled, mobile users would see accordion buttons but no content. This is inherent to the architecture and acceptable given the ARIA pattern requirements.

passedChecks: Build compiles without errors (webpack compiled successfully)
Block registers correctly with apiVersion 3 and proper attributes
Parent block (takt/tabs) and child block (takt/tabs-item) both properly configured
All required files exist for both parent and child blocks including frontend JS
All attributes defined with correct types and defaults
tabsHeading and tabsDescription sync logic implemented with useEffect
renameBlock() used for heading sync in both parent and child
Test page contains all 6 spec variations with correct content
ARIA tablist pattern with vertical orientation on desktop (role=tablist, role=tab, role=tabpanel)
ARIA accordion pattern on mobile (aria-expanded, role=region, aria-labelledby)
Keyboard navigation implemented (ArrowUp/Down, Home/End, Enter/Space)
Hidden + inert attributes on inactive panels and closed accordion content
Tabindex management correct (0 for active, -1 for inactive tabs)
shouldDisplay() used for ThemeHeading area conditional rendering
ThemeHeading with headingSize=3 and columns=2 matches spec
Tab description visibility toggled on active tab button
Plus/minus icon toggle implemented for mobile accordion
Content cloning from desktop panels to mobile accordion items
Responsive mode switching handles mobile-to-desktop transition (first tab selected if all closed)
Desktop-to-mobile transition opens currently selected tab's accordion
bg-accent used for active tab button and mobile open accordion
bg-accent-lighter used for desktop content panel
Inner blocks properly configured: parent allows only takt/tabs-item, child allows any blocks
Custom Appender component used for adding new tab items
addButtonToBlock used for child block toolbar settings button
TabsCurrent React context passes active tab index from parent to children
Auto-select newly added tabs in editor
SVG icons follow 24x24 grid with integer coordinates
